serviceId.meaning: stable deployment target and service configuration identity

deploymentId.meaning: one deployable/runtime version of the service
deploymentId.expectedOnRestore: a new deployment identity is normal

documentedActions.restart: restarts a process within a deployment
documentedActions.remove: stops the running deployment and marks it removed
documentedActions.redeploy: creates a fresh deployment using the selected deployment's source/configuration
documentedActions.regionMove: changes placement and may create new deployment/runtime identities
documentedActions.serviceReplacement: creates a materially different topology and is prohibited

outageGateIdentityRules: Redis service ID and service name must remain unchanged.
The environment-specific Redis service-instance ID must remain unchanged.
The logical volume ID and /data attachment must remain unchanged.
A new Redis deployment and runtime instance are expected during restoration and are not service replacement.
Web and worker deployment and process identities must remain unchanged.
No service creation, source upload, variable change, or region change is allowed.
